LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANTS SCOTTSDALE INSURANCE COMPANY, NATIONAL CASUALTY COMPANY, AND COOPER GAY RE, LTD'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. (1) DEMURRER WILL BE SUSTAINED WHERE THE DEFENDANT CANNOT REASONABLY RESPOND; I.E., HE OR SHE CANNOT REASONABLY DETERMINE WHAT ISSUES MUST BE ADMITTED OR DENIED, OR WHAT COUNTS OR CLAIMS ARE DIRECTED AGAINST HIM OR HER. (SEE KHOURY V. MALY'S OF CALIF., INC. (1993) 14 CA4TH 612, 616.) HERE, PLAINTIFF DOES NOT SPECIFICALLY NAME DEFENDANTS NATIONAL CASUALTY COMPANY OR COOPER GAY RE IN ASSOCIATION TO ANY CAUSE OF ACTION LISTED IN THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. THUS, PLAINTIFF HAS NOT PLEAD WITH SUFFICIENT CLARITY WHICH CAUSES OF ACTION ARE DIRECTED TOWARD DEFENDANTS NATIONAL CASUALTY COMPANY OR COOPER GAY RE. THEREFORE, WITH RESPECT TO DEFENDANTS NATIONAL CASUALTY COMPANY AND COOPER GAY RE, THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED, WITH 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. (2) IF A CONTRACT SET OUT IN THE COMPLAINT IS AMBIGUOUS, PLAINTIFF'S INTERPRETATION MUST BE ACCEPTED AS CORRECT IN TESTING THE SUFFICIENCY OF THE COMPLAINT. (SEE ARAGON-HAAS V. FAMILY SECURITY INS. SERVICES, INC. (1991) 231 CA3D 232, 239.) HERE, THE PARTIES INTERPRET THE CONTRACT DIFFERENTLY, THEREFORE, FOR PURPOSES OF DEMURRER, THE COURT MUST ACCEPT PLAINTIFF'S INTERPRETATION. THUS, THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED WITH RESPECT TO DEFENDANT SCOTTSDALE'S DEMURRER TO THE SECOND AND THIRD CAUSES OF ACTION. PREVAILING PARTY SHALL PREPARE FORM OF ORDER THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK FELICIA GREEN (302/EHG)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS SWETT & CRAWFORD, COOPER GAY RE AND STEPHANIE CASTRO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. THE MOVING DEFENDANTS DID NOT NEED TO SPEAK DIRECTLY TO PLAINTIFF TO BE LIABLE FOR FRAUD OR NEGLIGENT MISREPRESENTATION. 'THE MAKER OF A FRAUDULENT MISREPRESENTATION IS SUBJECT TO LIABILITY IF THE MISREPRESENTATION, ALTHOUGH NOT MADE DIRECTLY TO THE OTHER, IS MADE TO A THIRD PERSON AND THE MAKER INTENDS OR HAS REASON TO EXPECT THAT ITS TERMS WILL BE REPEATED OR ITS SUBSTANCE COMMUNICATED TO THE OTHER '." (HASSO V. HAPKE (2014) 227 CAL.APP.4TH 107, 130.) PLAINTIFF HAS PRESENTED EVIDENCE SHOWING THAT THERE ARE TRIABLE ISSUES REGARDING BOTH ALLEGED MISREPRESENTATIONS, INCLUDING WHETHER THE MISREPRESENTATIONS WERE MADE AND WERE TRUE AT THE TIME THEY WERE MADE, WHETHER THEY CAUSED ANY DAMAGE TO PLAINTIFF, AND PLAINTIFF JUSTIFIABLY RELIED ON THEM. (LAZAR DECLARATION). DEFENDANT DOES NOT ADDRESS ALL OF THE CLAIMS THAT WERE DENIED. (LAZAR DEC. PAR. 12.) MOREOVER, THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE WHETHER ALL OF THE LISTED VEHICLES WERE COVERED. (LAZAR DEC., PARS. 5 AND 11.) THE PREVAILING PARTY SHALL SUBMIT A [PROPOSED] WRITTEN ORDER THAT TRACKS THE COURT'S RULING, VERBATIM AND THAT COMPLIES WITH CCP 437C(G) AND CRC 3.1312. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Motion For Summary Adjudication By Defendants Scottsdale Insurance Company And National Casualty Company
Off Calendar Oct-06-2016 Continued To Dec-15-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANTS SCOTTSDALE INSURANCE COMPANY AND NATIONAL CASUALTY COMPANY MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Motion For Summary Adjudication is CONTINUED to December 15, 2016 at 9:30 AM in Department 302 to allow plaintiff Luxor Cabs, Inc. to conduct further discovery. (See CCP 437c(h).)

Notwithstanding the continuance of the hearing, the court provides some guidance for the parties based on the current record. The current record indicates that National Casualty is not a party to the insurance policy and was named in this lawsuit because of the inclusion of an erroneous signature page. However, Luxor should have the opportunity to conduct discovery to determine National Casualty's relationship to the policy. Luxor argues that Scottsdale breached the contract and the implied covenant by denying 6 claims. Based on the current record, it appears that there was no physical damage coverage for claims 1-5 based on the VIN numbers cited in the claims. The court notes that the VIN number for claim 2 (Defendant's UMF #13; Ex.C, Flores Dec.) is different by one digit from the number cited in endorsement 3. (Bates number 16964.) The inconsistency appears to be a typographical error. The court is not now persuaded by Luxor's argument that the claims involved vehicles listed in the original policy, which included physical damage protection. As to claim 6, the current record does not explain how Scottsdale can unilaterally remove physical damage coverage for that vehicle. (Lazar Dec par. 8.) Scottsdale's argument that it can cancel the policy for any reason-including pretextual reasons-is incorrect. Scottsdale overstates the holding of Jensen v. Traders & General Ins. Co. (1959) 52 Cal.2d 786. "Liability carriers do not have an unfettered right to cancel coverage, notwithstanding mutual cancellation clauses to that effect. Cancellation provisions in an insurance contract are subject to the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing just like any other clause." (Helfand v. National Union Fire Ins. Co. (1992) 10 Cal.App.4th 869, 903.) In all events, a continuance of the hearing is warranted to allow for the deposition of Mr. Shahri. Any supplemental opposition papers to be filed by Luxor must be filed no later than November 29, 2016. Any supplemental reply papers to be filed by Scottsdale and National Casualty must be filed no later than December 7, 2016.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Not Reported. (D302)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Oct-04-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, John W. Howard of JW Howard Attorneys (619.234.2842) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party, Luxor Cabs, Inc.

Attorney, Mark Inbody of Selman Breitman Law Offices (415.979.2051) appears as counsel for defendants/moving parties, Sweet & Crawford, Cooper Gay Re, and Stephanie Castro

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANTS SWETT & CRAWFORD, COOPER GAY RE and STEPHANIE CASTRO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is DENIED. The moving defendants did not need to speak directly to plaintiff to be liable for fraud or negligent misrepresentation. 'The maker of a fraudulent misrepresentation is subject to liability ' if the misrepresentation, although not made directly to the other, is made to a third person and the maker intends or has reason to expect that its terms will be repeated or its substance communicated to the other '." (Hasso v. Hapke (2014) 227 Cal.App.4th 107, 130.) Plaintiff has presented evidence showing that there are triable issues regarding both alleged misrepresentations, including whether the misrepresentations were made and were true at the time they were made, whether they caused any damage to plaintiff, and plaintiff justifiably relied on them. (Lazar Declaration). Defendant does not address all of the claims that were denied. (Lazar Dec. par. 12.) Moreover, there is a triable issue whether all of the listed vehicles were covered. (Lazar Dec., pars. 5 and 11.) The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with CCP 437c(g) and CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Sherry L. Sawyer, CSR No. 5976, email: ssawyer@sftc.org. (D302)

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-29-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances in the matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANTS SCOTTSDALE INSURANCE COMPANY, NATIONAL CASUALTY COMPANY, AND COOPER GAY RE, LTD'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained in part and overruled in part. (1) Demurrer will be sustained where the defendant cannot reasonably respond; i.e., he or she cannot reasonably determine what issues must be admitted or denied, or what counts or claims are directed against him or her. (See Khoury v. Maly's of Calif., Inc. (1993) 14 CA4th 612, 616.) Here, Plaintiff does not specifically name Defendants National Casualty Company or Cooper Gay Re in association to any cause of action listed in the Second Amended Complaint. Thus, Plaintiff has not plead with sufficient clarity which causes of action are directed toward Defendants National Casualty Company or Cooper Gay Re. Therefore, with respect to Defendants National Casualty Company and Cooper Gay Re, the demurrer is sustained, with 20 days leave to amend. (2) If a contract set out in the complaint is ambiguous, plaintiff's interpretation must be accepted as correct in testing the sufficiency of the complaint. (See Aragon-Haas v. Family Security Ins. Services, Inc. (1991) 231 CA3d 232, 239.) Here, the parties interpret the contract differently, therefore, for purposes of demurrer, the Court must accept Plaintiff's interpretation. Thus, the demurrer is overruled with respect to Defendant Scottsdale's demurrer to the second and third causes of action. Prevailing party shall prepare form of order that complies with CRC 3.1312. Judge Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk Felicia Green; Dept. 302. (302/EHG)
